metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the autodeploy self-update exclusion note: clarify that `autodeploy.sh` itself *does* auto-update via rsync; it is `install-autodeploy.sh` and the `rebar-autodeploy.{service,timer}` unit files that are excluded from in-run re-materialization and require hand-deployment.
evidence: infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh lines 74–93: 'autodeploy's OWN installer/self-update (install-autodeploy.sh + the rebar-autodeploy.{service,timer} unit files it writes) is DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED from in-run re-materialization — there is intentionally NO AUTODEPLOY_PATHS block'

infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh lines 92–93: '(The script BODY, autodeploy.sh, still updates in place via the BOT_PATHS rsync like any other source; this exclusion is specifically about the installer/unit-file self-update.)'
Plan phrase: 'a one-line note that autodeploy.sh is excluded from self-update (must be hand-deployed)'
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'autodeploy.sh is excluded from self-update (must be hand-deployed)' but infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh lines 74–93 and 92–93 document that it is the INSTALLER (install-autodeploy.sh) and systemd unit files that are excluded from in-run re-materialization — not autodeploy.sh itself. The script body of autodeploy.sh still auto-updates in place via the BOT_PATHS rsync. Encoding the imprecise phrasing into the runbook will mislead operators into thinking the whole autodeploy mechanism needs hand-deploys when only the installer/unit-file layer does.
findings.location: ## What — 'a one-line note that autodeploy.sh is excluded from self-update (must be hand-deployed)'
scenarios: An operator reads the new runbook note, thinks a change to autodeploy.sh requires a manual SSH/SSM session to copy the script, and either skips a critical script update or performs an unnecessary manual step.
The note in the runbook contradicts the inline comments in autodeploy.sh, creating conflicting documentation that erodes trust in both.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'autodeploy.sh is excluded from self-update (must be hand-deployed)' with: 'Note: `autodeploy.sh` (the script body) auto-updates in place on every deploy. The autodeploy *installer* (`install-autodeploy.sh`) and its systemd unit files (`rebar-autodeploy.{service,timer}`) are excluded from in-run re-materialization and must be hand-applied via a direct SSM session when changed.'

evidence: 1 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
no verification commands or testing plan referenced
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

evidence: file_impact is empty
findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

AC1 covers: instance id + region + terraform trace
AC2 covers: SSM connect commands
AC3 covers: docs-only change
ABSENCE: no AC references autodeploy.sh or self-update exclusion
findings.finding: The 'What' section describes a deliverable — 'a one-line note that autodeploy.sh is excluded from self-update (must be hand-deployed)' — that has no covering acceptance criterion. An operator reading only the ACs would have no basis to verify this content was added.
scenarios: A reviewer checking only the ACs approves the PR without the autodeploy.sh note; the runbook is incomplete and an operator hand-deploying autodeploy.sh has no runbook guidance.
findings.suggested_fix: Add: '- [ ] Includes a note that autodeploy.sh is excluded from self-update and must be deployed manually (not via the deploy pipeline).' as a fourth AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC line verifying the autodeploy.sh hand-deploy note is present (e.g. 'runbook notes that autodeploy.sh must be hand-deployed and is excluded from self-update').
evidence: Plan 'What' text: 'a one-line note that autodeploy.sh is excluded from self-update (must be hand-deployed)'
AC list has three items (instance id + region with terraform trace, SSM connect commands, docs-only change); none mentions autodeploy.sh
findings.finding: The 'What' section names a non-deferred deliverable — 'a one-line note that autodeploy.sh is excluded from self-update (must be hand-deployed)' — but no Acceptance Criterion covers it. An implementer who omits this note passes all three ACs and the goal goes unverified.
scenarios: An implementer adds the instance ID, region, and SSM snippets, passes all three ACs, and never adds the autodeploy note — the operational hazard the author wanted documented goes missing with no failing check.

Add to Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Includes a note that autodeploy.sh is excluded from self-update and must be hand-deployed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each AC, e.g.: AC1+AC2: `grep -E 'i-00880b2c7f13527c5|us-east-1|ssm start-session' infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md` exits 0; AC3: `git diff --name-only HEAD~1 | grep -vE '\.md$'` exits 1 (no non-.md files changed).
evidence: AC1: 'infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md names the prod instance id AND region up front…' — no grep/diff command stated
AC2: 'Includes the SSM connect command(s)' — no proving command stated
AC3: 'Docs-only change; no behavior change' — no diff filter command stated
ABSENCE: plan contains zero shell commands in the verification section
findings.finding: None of the three acceptance criteria carry a concrete proving command; a reviewer must infer how to verify each one.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria section (all three AC lines)
scenarios: An automated agent or a future reviewer checks the PR and has no canonical command to confirm the instance id and region are present and correctly placed.
AC3 ('no behavior change') is asserted but there is no stated check to confirm only .md files changed.
findings.suggested_fix:
Append a 'Verification' block to the plan:
```
## Verification
# AC1 + AC2 — instance id, region, and SSM snippet present
grep -cE 'i-00880b2c7f13527c5' infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md   # expect ≥1
grep -cE 'us-east-1' infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md             # expect ≥1
grep -cE 'ssm (start-session|send-command)' infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md  # expect ≥1

# AC3 — docs-only diff
git diff --name-only origin/main | grep -vE '\.md$'  # expect empty output
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a note alongside the instance id explaining it changes if the EC2 instance is recreated, and where to find the current id (e.g., `terraform output instance_id` or `aws ec2 describe-instances --filters Name=tag:Name,Values=review-bot`) so the runbook stays self-healing.
evidence: Plan states region is 'traced to var.aws_region / versions.tf as the source of truth' but makes no equivalent statement for the instance id
Instance id is sourced from ADR-0026, which is also a static doc with no auto-update mechanism
EC2 instance ids change on instance replacement (terraform destroy+apply), making the runbook entry stale without any detection mechanism
T7(b): change could strand stale reference if the box is ever recreated
T5e(b): a value expected to possibly evolve (instance id changes on recreation) is hardcoded without a changeability note
findings.finding: The instance id `i-00880b2c7f13527c5` is hardcoded in the runbook with no living source of truth and no note about how to update it when the EC2 instance is replaced, creating a future stale-reference risk. The plan correctly traces the region to `var.aws_region`/`versions.tf`, but applies no equivalent traceability to the instance id — ADR-0026 (cited as the source) is itself a static document, not a live artifact.
findings.location: ## What — 'Add a The box (where prod runs) reference section … instance i-00880b2c7f13527c5' / AC line 1
scenarios: Operator runs the hardcoded SSM connect snippet after the box was recreated by terraform — session fails silently or errors with 'TargetNotConnected', and the runbook gives no guidance on where to find the current id
Runbook drifts from reality over time with no mechanism to notice
findings.suggested_fix: After the instance id line, add: `> ⚠️ Instance ids change on recreation. To verify the current id: aws ec2 describe-instances --filters 'Name=tag:Name,Values=review-bot' --query 'Reservations[].Instances[].InstanceId' --output text` (or equivalent terraform output). Update this runbook when the instance is replaced.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Pin the exact flag names for both snippets in the plan/AC: `aws ssm start-session --target i-00880b2c7f13527c5 --region us-east-1` and `aws ssm send-command --instance-ids i-00880b2c7f13527c5 --region us-east-1 --document-name AWS-RunShellScript --parameters commands=["..."]` (verify each flag against `aws ssm start-session help` / `aws ssm send-command help` before authoring).
evidence: `aws ssm start-session` uses `--target <instance-id>` (not `--instance-id`); `aws ssm send-command` uses `--instance-ids` (plural) — flag-level detail unspecified in the plan
Existing runbook line 298 references `aws ssm send-command … AWS-RunShellScript` only in prose, never as a full runnable snippet with flags
Evidence class: Partially-verified — integration exists, specific flag names recalled from training but not observed from `--help` output in this review session
findings.finding: The plan specifies adding `aws ssm start-session` and `aws ssm send-command` connect snippets but does not fix the flag names to be used. `aws ssm start-session` takes `--target <instance-id>` while `aws ssm send-command` takes `--instance-ids` (plural), not `--instance-id`. Writing the wrong flag name (a common gotcha) produces a CLI error at the exact moment an operator needs the runbook most. The plan's AC only says 'Includes the SSM connect command(s)' without pinning the flag-level form, leaving the authored snippet unverified.
findings.location: ## What — 'the 'aws ssm start-session'/'send-command' connect snippets'; AC line 2 — 'Includes the SSM connect command(s)'
scenarios: Operator pastes the `send-command` snippet during an incident and gets 'Unknown options: --instance-id' because the plan author wrote the singular form — delaying remediation at the worst time.
Operator uses `start-session` with `--instance-id` (wrong) instead of `--target` (correct) and gets a validation error.
findings.suggested_fix:
Add a concrete, flag-verified snippet to the plan's ## What section:
```bash
# Interactive shell
aws ssm start-session --target i-00880b2c7f13527c5 --region us-east-1

# Non-interactive one-liner
aws ssm send-command \
  --instance-ids i-00880b2c7f13527c5 \
  --region us-east-1 \
  --document-name AWS-RunShellScript \
  --parameters commands=["sudo journalctl -u rebar-autodeploy.service --no-pager | tail -50"]
```
Note: `start-session` uses `--target`; `send-command` uses `--instance-ids` (plural).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-line auth precondition note to the new 'The box' section stating the operator IAM requirement (e.g. `ssm:StartSession` / `ssm:SendCommand`) and that the SSM Agent must be healthy on the instance.
evidence: Plan says 'Add a 'The box (where prod runs)' reference section … the 'aws ssm start-session'/'send-command' connect snippets' but states no auth preconditions for the operator side
Existing runbook has no SSM auth guidance anywhere in the file
infra/terraform/iam.tf contains SSM-related instance-role policies but operator-IAM requirement is separate and unmentioned
Evidence class: Unverified — no probe of actual operator IAM policy in the account
findings.finding: The plan does not state operator-side auth preconditions for SSM Session Manager: the operator must have IAM permissions `ssm:StartSession` (and `ssm:SendCommand` for send-command), the SSM Agent must be running on the instance, and the instance role must have `AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore` or equivalent. Without this, an operator following the new runbook section may get an `AccessDeniedException` or a session-refused error and have no guidance on why.
findings.location: ## What — 'aws ssm start-session'/'send-command' connect snippets'; AC — 'Includes the SSM connect command(s)'
scenarios: A new operator follows the runbook, runs `aws ssm start-session --target i-...` and gets `AccessDeniedException: User is not authorized to perform: ssm:StartSession` with no hint in the runbook about what permission is needed.
